methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rivian is a publicly traded transportation company founded by R. J. Scaringe with a current market capitalization of $20.7 billion and total capital raised of $13.5 billion. The Transportation sector peer universe consists of five identified private companies—The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace—with no directly comparable public peers available for valuation triangulation.

The Peer-Median method was not applicable to this analysis. The Transportation sector contains only four same-sector peers, but applying stage-specific and valuation-tier filters yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for a reliable multiple-based valuation. A cross-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given the specialized nature of frontier transportation technology and capital intensity of the sector.

As a result, this analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range supported by comparable company multiples. The narrow peer set and absence of close-stage, similarly-sized public comps prevent standardized relative valuation. Any assessment of Rivian's $20.7 billion valuation against intrinsic value would require application of fundamentals-based methods—discounted cash flow analysis or sum-of-the-parts modeling—rather than market multiples. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to the private peer cohort, path to profitability, and execution risk on announced production targets.
